revision.comment: Bot: mark 1 deleted QID(s) as DELETED_QID in ill templates [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/24618454543|time triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:穂見諏訪十五所神社]]
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Homi Suwa Jugosho Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Homisuwajyugosyo-jinja-haiden.jpg |location = {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|en|Yamanashi Prefecture|qid=Q132720|lt=Yamanashi Prefecture|1=Yamanashi Prefecture}}{{ill|Hokuto, Yamanashi|en|Hokuto, Yamanashi|qid=Q825272|lt=Hokuto, Yamanashi|1=Hokuto, Yamanashi}}, Nagasaka Town, Kamijo Miyakubo 1461 |coordinates = {{Coord|35.821|N|138.37247222222223|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity = {{ill|Ukemochi|en|Ukemochi|qid=Q2280323|lt=Ukemochi|1=Ukemochi}}, among others |consecration_year = Unknown |Festival = {{ill|April 10|en|April 10|qid=Q2502|lt=April 10|1=April 10}}<ref name="NagasakaChronic">{{Cite book|author=Nagasaka Town Historical Compilation Committee |title=Nagasaka Town Chronicle, Volume II |publisher=Nagasaka Town |date=1990 |page=947}}</ref>, {{ill|October 9|en|October 9|qid=Q2923|lt=October 9|1=October 9}}<ref>{{Cite book|author=Yamanashi Nichinichi Newspaper |title=Yamanashi Encyclopedia |publisher=Yamanashi Nichinichi Newspaper |date=1972 |page=852}}</ref> |Rituals = Tubokayu Ritual |Cultural Assets = City-designated cultural property: main shrine, Tubokayu Ritual, and sacred zelkova tree |location = {{ill|Hokuto|qid=Q825272|1=Hokuto, Yamanashi}} }}
'''Homi Suwa Jugosho Shrine''' (穂見諏訪十五所神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|qid=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Hokuto, Yamanashi|en|Hokuto, Yamanashi|qid=Q825272|lt=Hokuto, Yamanashi|1=Hokuto, Yamanashi}}, Nagasaka Town, Kamijo Miyakubo 1461, in {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|en|Yamanashi Prefecture|qid=Q132720|lt=Yamanashi Prefecture|1=Yamanashi Prefecture}}. The shrine was previously classified as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|qid=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|qid=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q712534}}. Its main shrine, Tubokayu ritual, and a large zelkova tree on its grounds are designated as city cultural assets.<ref name="HokutoCityOverview2014">{{Cite book|author=Hokuto City |title=Hokuto City Overview 2014 |publisher=Hokuto City |date=2014}}</ref> The ''Kai Kokushi'' records the shrine’s estate as "4 koku and 7 to of stamped land, shrine grounds, mountain forest, and 3 tan and 2 se of paddies."<ref>''Kai Kokushi,'' Vol. 3. Yūzankaku, 1982, p. 97.</ref>
== Deities == The shrine enshrines multiple deities due to numerous amalgamations, with three significant kami: * {{ill|Ukemochi|en|Ukemochi|qid=Q2280323|lt=Ukemochi|1=Ukemochi}} * {{ill|Takeminakata no Mikoto|ja|建御名方命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeminakata no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タケミナカタ|1=Takeminakata|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q9150391}} * {{ill|Five Deities of Heaven, Earth, and Man|ja|天地人五行神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Five Deities of Heaven, Earth, and Man|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=DELETED_QID|1=Q135579030}}
== History == === Amalgamation of Shrines === Homi Suwa Jugosho Shrine was formed by the amalgamation of multiple shrines. It is speculated to have been founded in the {{ill|Zhenguan|en|Zhenguan|qid=Q3577278|lt=Jōgan era|1=Zhenguan}}, 6th year (864). The ''Engishiki'' completed in {{ill|913|en|913|qid=Q24860|lt=913|1=913}} mentions a "Homi Shrine in Kai Province," identifying it as an {{ill|Ancient shrine|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ancient shrine|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}.<ref>{{Cite book|title=Nagasaka Homeland |publisher=Nagasaka Town Local Studies Association |date=2008 |pages=11–15}}</ref>
In the 13th century, a shrine official was established, and the monk Ryūzō resided there, practicing esoteric rites and reportedly engaging in doctrinal debates with {{ill|Nichiren|1=Nichiren|2=ja|3=日蓮|4=ko|5=니치렌|6=de|7=Nichiren|8=es|9=Nichiren|10=fr|11=Nichiren|12=it|13=Nichiren|14=nl|15=Nichiren|16=no|17=Nichiren|lt=Nichiren|qid=Q311478ja|Nichiren|1=Nichiren|2=ja|3=日蓮|4=ko|5=니치렌|6=de|7=Nichiren|8=es|9=Nichiren|10=fr|11=Nichiren|12=it|13=Nichiren|14=nl|15=Nichiren|16=no|17=Nichiren|lt=Nichiren|qid=Q31147812=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nichiren|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. By 1275, the {{ill|Wuxing (Chinese philosophy)|en|Wuxing (Chinese philosophy)|qid=Q218652|lt=Five Elements (Wuxing)|1=Wuxing (Chinese philosophy)}} (Five Elements theory) influenced the shrine's practices.
During the {{ill|Tenmon era|ja|天文|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmon era|1=Tenmon|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q21096812}} famine (1534–1547), {{ill|Takeda Nobutora|en|Takeda Nobutora|qid=Q1192628|lt=Takeda Nobutora|1=Takeda Nobutora}} ordered the Tubokayu ritual to ensure agricultural prosperity. The shrine's current amalgamated form dates to this period.
== Architecture and Grounds == The shrine covers 848 tsubo. A {{ill|Natural monument|en|Natural monument|qid=Q23790|lt=natural monument|1=Natural monument}} zelkova tree is located within the shrine grounds, alongside an ancient main shrine reconstructed in the Edo period.<ref>{{Cite book|title=Kyohoku Shrine Chronicle |publisher=Kyohoku Shrine Council |date=1962}}</ref>
== Rituals == === Tubokayu Ritual === The shrine’s Tubokayu divination ritual has been practiced since the 16th century, using rice porridge to predict agricultural outcomes.<ref>{{Cite book|title=Nagasaka Homeland |publisher=Nagasaka Town Local Studies Association |date=1988 |pages=62–66}}</ref>
== Cultural Assets == Three items are designated as cultural assets: * Main Shrine: Rebuilt in the Edo period. * Sacred Zelkova Tree: Estimated to be over 700 years old. * Tubokayu Ritual: A unique divination practice carried out during {{ill|First Full Moon Festival|en|First Full Moon Festival|qid=Q13423452|lt=Koshogatsu|1=First Full Moon Festival}}.
== Access == * Located at {{ill|Hokuto, Yamanashi|en|Hokuto, Yamanashi|qid=Q825272|lt=Hokuto, Yamanashi|1=Hokuto, Yamanashi}}, Nagasaka Town * Accessible via JR Nagasaka Station (10 minutes on foot) or by car from Nagasaka IC (5 minutes).
== References == {{Reflist|2}}
== External Links == * [http://www.yamanashi-jinjacho.or.jp/intro/search/detail/6164 Homi Suwa Jugosho Shrine] – Yamanashi Shrine Agency * [https://www.yamanashi-kankou.jp/rekitabi/jisha/spot/306.html Homi Suwa Jugosho Shrine] – Yamanashi Historical Tourism
{{translated page|ja|穂見諏訪十五所神社|version=99632457|comment=Imported full ja history}}
[[ja:穂見諏訪十五所神社]]
